This I say, brethren, that <na>[1]</na> flesh and blood cannot possess the Kingdom of God: neither shall corruption possess incorruption.

notes.text: Flesh and blood signify not here the substance of those things, but the corrupt quality incident to them in this life by the fall of Adam.

verses.text: <na>[1]</na> Behold I tell you a mystery. We shall all indeed rise again: but we shall not all be changed.

notes.text: b The Epistle upon All-Souls day.

verses.text: In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the <cr>[1]</cr> last trumpet (for <cr>[2]</cr> the trumpet shall sound) and the dead shall rise again incorruptible: and we shall be changed.
For this corruptible must do on incorruption; & this mortal do on immortality.
And when this mortal hath done on immortality, then shall come to pass the saying that is written: [1] Death is swallowed up in victory.
Death where is thy victory? Death where is thy sting?
And the sting of death, is sin: and the power of sin is the Law.
But thanks be to God that hath given us the victory by our Lord <sc>Jesus</sc> Christ.
Therefore, my beloved brethren, be stable and unmoveable; abounding in the work of our Lord always, knowing that your labour is not vain in our Lord:

chapters.summary: He proveth the resurrection of the dead by the resurrection of Christ, and with many other arguments: and 31. answereth also objections made against it. 49. And then exhorteth in respect of it, unto good life. <na>[1]</na>

summary_notes.text: The 7. part. Of the resurrection of the dead.

verses.text: <sc>And</sc> concerning the collections that are made for the Saints, as I have ordained to the Churches of Galatia, so do ye also.
In <na>[1]</na> the first of the Sabboth let every one of you put a part with himself, laying up what shall well like him: that not when I come, then collections be made.

notes.text: That is Sunday. Hiero. q. 4. Hedibiæ. So quickly did the Christians keep Sunday, holiday, and assembled to Divine Service on the same.

verses.text: And when I shall be present; whom you shall approve by letters, them will I send to carry your grace into Jerusalem.
And if it be worthy that I also go, they shall go with me.
And I will come to you, when I shall have passed through Macedonia. For I will pass through Macedonia.
And with you perhaps I will abide, or will winter also: that you may bring me on my way whithersoever I go.
For I will not now see you by the way, for I hope that I shall abide with you some little time, if our Lord will permit.
But I will tarry at Ephesus until <na>[1]</na> Pentecost.

notes.text: The Heretics & other new-fangled strive among themselves, whether Pentecost signify here the term of fifty days; or else the Jews' holy-day so called. But it cometh not to their minds that it is most like to be the feast of Whitsuntide, kept & instituted even then by the Apostles, as appeareth by the Fathers. See S. Aug. ep. 119. c. 15. & 16. Ambr. in c. 17. Lucæ.

verses.text: For a great door and evident is opened unto me: and many adversaries.
And if Timothee come, see that he be without fear with you, for he worketh the work of our Lord, as also I.
Let no man therefore despise him, but conduct ye him in peace: that he may come to me. For I expect him with the brethren.

And of brother Apollo I do you to understand, that I much entreated him to come unto you with the brethren: and <na>[1]</na> at all it was not his mind to come now. But he will come when he shall have leisure.

notes.text: c πάντως

verses.text: Watch ye, stand in the faith, do manfully, & be strengthened.
Let all your things be done in charity.
And I beseech you, brethren, you know the house of Stephanas, and of Fortunatus, that they are the first fruits of Achaia, & have ordained themselves to the ministry of the saints:
that you also be subject to such, and to every one that helpeth & laboureth with us.
And I rejoice in the presence of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because <na>[1]</na> that which you wanted, they have supplied.

notes.text: c τὸ ὑμῶν ὑςέρημα.

verses.text: For they have refreshed both my spirit and yours. Know them therefore that are such.
The Churches of Asia salute you. Aquila and Priscilla with their domestical Church salute you much in our Lord.
All the brethren salute you. Salute one another in a <cr>[1]</cr> holy kiss.
The salutation with mine own hand Paul's.
If any man love not our Lord Jesus Christ, be he anathema. [1][2] Maran atha.

notes.text: c That is, our Lord is come. Hiero. ep. 173. Therefore anathema to all that love him not, or believe not. Theophyla. upon this place.
c אתא מרן

verses.text: The grace of our Lord <sc>Jesus</sc> Christ be with you.
My charity be with you all in Christ <sc>Jesus</sc>. Amen.

chapters.summary: He prescribeth an order for their contributing to the Christians at Hierusalem, 3. promising to come unto them. 10. Of Timothee, and of Apollo's coming thither. 13. And so with exhortation, and diverse commendations, he endeth. <na>[1]</na>
